methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A chip and semiconductor company founded by James and Oliver Proud, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $100M+ in prior raises. The peer universe comprises 3 public semiconductor companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip firms, including stage-matched comparables Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression anchors to the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $2.0B. Peer-median triangulation across the 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies produces a median of $2.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied, as Substrate's revenue is not disclosed—a standard constraint for private-company valuation.

These two applicable methods converge on a $2.0B median with a triangulated range of $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high). The median aligns precisely with both approaches, reducing model risk. Key uncertainties include unit economics relative to peers, capital efficiency relative to burn, and near-term revenue trajectory—all material drivers of Series B and later-stage multiples in this sector.
